codeReview.summary: The F012-BE Sync Status Tracking Backend implementation is functionally complete and covers all required feature behaviors: schema extensions for sync_status tracking, IPC handlers for real-time updates, and integration with the sync queue via callbacks. The worker followed AGENTS.md conventions (Indonesian messages, IPC channel naming, return types) and the backend-worker skill procedure. TypeScript compilation passes and 23 new unit tests were added. No blocking issues were found.

issues.description: addSyncStatusColumns() references a non-existent `addColumn` method on kasDb/bankDb/gudangDb. It falls back gracefully to a success message, but this fallback means the migration utility cannot actually add columns at runtime if needed outside of normal DB init. The columns are already added at init time in each module database, so this is not a blocking issue for current usage.

integrateWithSyncQueue() is a no-op placeholder. The actual integration path is handleSyncQueueItemCompleted(), but it is unclear from the code whether batchSyncService or retryService actually call this function. As a result, automatic status transitions on sync queue completion may not trigger in production unless an upstream caller was added in a later feature (F014-BE touches sync health). The IPC handlers allow manual updates, so UI-driven flows still work.

Tests are mostly shallow (unknown-module error paths and constant checks). They do not exercise the real database module integration for kas/bank/gudang because the module-private db pattern makes unit testing sql.js-backed functions difficult. This is consistent with prior backend features in this codebase, but coverage of the actual UPDATE/SELECT paths is minimal.

PRAGMA table_info parsing uses `.columns.indexOf('name')` inside a `some()` callback. `indexOf` on the array returned by sql.js may not exist if the column metadata shape differs. The code catches and logs failures, so it's safe, but hardening the column-existence check would be better.

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker SKILL.md says to write database-operation tests first (TDD), but the worker wrote mostly constant/error-path tests because the module-private db variables in kasDatabase.ts, bankDatabase.ts, and gudangDatabase.ts make it impossible to unit-test sql.js-backed functions without heavy mocking or integration-level database setup. The skill should either acknowledge this limitation or recommend an integration-test pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: F012-BE test file only tests unknown-module guards and Indonesian message constants; no actual database UPDATE/SELECT assertions. Same pattern exists in F010-BE and F014-BE.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no explicit npm script for end-to-end backend verification of sync-queue-to-transaction-status integration. The worker used `npm test -- --grep 'sync-status'` in verification, but no such grep pattern exists in the test output; tests are under `SyncStatusTracking` describe blocks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verificationSteps references `npm test -- --grep 'sync-status'`, but the actual test suite uses `describe('SyncStatusTracking', ...)` without the hyphen. The tests pass regardless because Jest runs the whole file.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The sync status enum in localDatabaseManager.ts uses 'error' while kas/bank/gudangDatabase.ts and syncStatusTracking.ts use 'failed'. This inconsistency is pre-existing and not introduced by F012-BE, but AGENTS.md does not document which enum values are canonical per table type.

The codebase has two parallel database paths: localDatabaseManager.ts (schema-first, pooled) and the legacy module databases (kasDatabase.ts, bankDatabase.ts, gudangDatabase.ts) which manage their own sql.js instances. Sync status tracking was added to the legacy module databases, which is the correct choice because the renderer and existing IPC infrastructure depend on them. This architectural split is worth documenting for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: F012-BE adds sync columns to kas/bank/gudang transaction tables and exposes them through those modules’ exported functions, rather than through localDatabaseManager.

summary: F012-BE passes review. The implementation correctly extends transaction schemas with sync tracking fields, adds IPC handlers for real-time updates, and provides the callback hooks needed for sync-queue integration. Minor non-blocking gaps: the no-op integrateWithSyncQueue placeholder, shallow unit-test coverage of real DB paths, and a non-existent addColumn fallback in the migration helper. No blocking bugs or convention violations.
